_comment: Ranker-regression expectations over data/seed.json + data/cooccurrence.json. CHARACTERIZATION values (what the deterministic engine produces for the default JOURNEY archetype at the trust>=0.50 gate), not hand-picked winners. Update deliberately when the seed, co-occurrence, or scoring changes, and record why. History: science subgraph -> Mithra; Hellenistic-India-Buddhism bridge (0011); default cap cut 6->4 (0012); breadth clusters Greece (0016), Egypt (0017), Islamic Golden Age (0018), Scientific Revolution (0019), East Asia (0020); journey cap cut 4->3 (0021, fixed-length 3-hop journeys); Norse/Celtic (0022); Chinese tech (0023); second-order co-occurrence (0025), reworked by full-link Jaccard similarity (0029) which measures shared context over each article's whole outbound link set rather than the seed-sized keyhole -- this de-saturated the term (max tie-fraction 94% -> 1.1%) and re-ordered the endpoints again. ADR 0034 weighted each domain jump by its unexpectedness given the predicate (a flat count paid full price for tautological crossings like located_in->geography) and ADR 0035 closed the 24 open-ended temporal extents (a still-standing city founded in 59 BC no longer reads as an ancient node). ADR 0038 added the South/SE Asia cluster (9 nodes / 17 statements): the new edges shifted predicate-level rarity globally, re-ordering two winners hair's-breadth apart -- Roman Empire's top journey flipped Qin Shi Huang (via Silk Road -> Great Wall) -> Plato (via Ancient Greece -> Renaissance humanism), 38.02 vs 37.65, and Euclid's terminus moved from Persia to the new Maurya Empire (Euclid -> Alexandria -> Alexander the Great -> Maurya Empire). Christianity -> Paper is unchanged. Re-characterise after any seed/co-occurrence/scoring change; winners were re-characterised from the engine -- not hand-picked.
